title: Optimize Salesforce Metadata Refresh Batch Sizes for Performance
story: As a DataTools Pro user refreshing Salesforce metadata in the Data Dictionary, I want the refresh process to complete faster so that it does not feel slow or broken when syncing field changes from Salesforce.
description:
The Salesforce metadata refresh process makes multiple sequential passes to Salesforce to retrieve metadata, record data, and then assess changes. Users reported the process felt slow and appeared broken.

## Optimization Areas
1. Review and optimize batch sizes for Salesforce API requests when pulling updated field data
2. Consider a modal UX pattern (similar to Metrics Analyst) to provide feedback during the refresh process
3. Optimize the order of operations for metadata → records → change detection
acceptance_criteria: Salesforce metadata refresh completes noticeably faster than before optimization
Batch sizes for Salesforce API requests are tuned for performance
User receives feedback during the refresh process indicating progress
Refresh process handles large object field lists without timeout or failure
ux_changes: Improved perceived performance of the metadata refresh process. Consider modal pattern for progress feedback during long-running refresh operations.
middleware_changes: Optimized batch sizes and request sequencing for Salesforce metadata retrieval. Reduced unnecessary API calls and improved the order of operations for metadata sync.
implementation_notes: Code was optimized and deployed to the development environment. Initial testing confirmed improved speed. The approach focused on batch size tuning rather than architectural changes.
comment_summary: Waqar optimized the code and deployed to the dev environment. Ryan confirmed it felt faster on first test.
